const { main } = require('/home/runner/work/_temp/gh-aw/actions/compute_text.cjs');
await main();

2026-04-20T23:07:55.1041325Z   github-token: ***
2026-04-20T23:07:55.1041707Z   debug: false
2026-04-20T23:07:55.1042088Z   user-agent: actions/github-script
2026-04-20T23:07:55.1042631Z   result-encoding: json
2026-04-20T23:07:55.1043024Z   retries: 0
2026-04-20T23:07:55.1043430Z   retry-exempt-status-codes: 400,401,403,404,422
2026-04-20T23:07:55.1043948Z ##[endgroup]
2026-04-20T23:07:55.4896974Z Repository permission level: write
2026-04-20T23:07:55.4962949Z Redacted URL: crates.io
2026-04-20T23:07:55.4971176Z Redacted URL: crates.io
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5003731Z Redacted URL: crates.io
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5052008Z Redacted URL: crates.io
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5058765Z text: Create migration guides for all official Azure SDK for Rust crates
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5059422Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5059916Z ## Create migration guides for all official Azure SDK for Rust crates
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5100724Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5101636Z We need migration guides to help users transition from unofficial/community crates and older SDK versions to the official Azure SDK for Rust libraries.
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5102616Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5102773Z ### Background
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5102988Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5103454Z The Azure SDK for Rust has gone through three eras:
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5103849Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5104891Z 1. **Community era (2017–2019):** MindFlavor's `azure_sdk_*` crates ([MindFlavor/AzureSDKForRust](https://github.com/MindFlavor/AzureSDKForRust))
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5107917Z 2. **Experimental era (2022–2024):** Azure-published `azure_*` crates, v0.1–0.21 (not stable)
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5109051Z 3. **Official/GA era (2025+):** Current rewrite with stable API design, new crate names for several services
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5109711Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5110611Z Many Rust developers are using crates from eras 1 or 2, or community alternatives like [`azeventhubs`]((crates.io/redacted)(crates.io/redacted) Each migration guide should cover:
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5113189Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5113579Z - What the predecessor crate was and its relationship to the official crate
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5114306Z - Key API differences and breaking changes
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5114912Z - Step-by-step migration instructions with code examples
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5115785Z - Common pitfalls and FAQ
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5116068Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5116234Z ### Sub-Issues
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5116446Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5116935Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_core` (from azure_core ≤0.21.0 / azure_sdk_core) — #TBD1
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5118009Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_identity` (from azure_identity ≤0.21.0 / azure_sdk_auth_aad) — #TBD2
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5119120Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_security_keyvault_secrets` (from azure_security_keyvault) — #TBD3
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5120210Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_security_keyvault_keys` (from azure_security_keyvault) — #TBD4
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5121335Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_security_keyvault_certificates` (from azure_security_keyvault) — #TBD5
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5122355Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_messaging_eventhubs` (from azeventhubs) — #TBD6
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5123364Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_data_cosmos` (from azure_data_cosmos ≤0.21.0 / azure_sdk_cosmos) — #TBD7
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5124524Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_storage_blob` (from azure_storage_blobs / azure_sdk_storage_blob) — #TBD8
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5126699Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_storage_queue` (from azure_storage_queues) — #TBD9
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5127319Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5128194Z > **Note:** Migration guide for `azure_messaging_servicebus` is deferred until the era 3 rewrite is published on crates.io.
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5128975Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5129130Z ### Priority
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5129334Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5132153Z 1. **GA crates first (April 2026):** Identity, Key Vault (Secrets, Keys, Certificates), Event Hubs, Storage Blob
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5133349Z 2. **Near-GA:** Core (infrastructure for all crates), Cosmos DB (June 2026), Storage Queue
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5134206Z 3. **Deferred:** Service Bus (era 3 rewrite not yet published)
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5134639Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5134804Z ### Acceptance Criteria
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5135077Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5135262Z - [ ] All 9 sub-issues filed and linked
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5136076Z - [ ] Each migration guide published in repo docs
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5136677Z - [ ] Each guide includes code samples for key scenarios
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5137290Z - [ ] Each guide linked from the relevant crate README
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5138015Z title: Create migration guides for all official Azure SDK for Rust crates
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5138838Z body: ## Create migration guides for all official Azure SDK for Rust crates
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5139346Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5140110Z We need migration guides to help users transition from unofficial/community crates and older SDK versions to the official Azure SDK for Rust libraries.
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5141025Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5141181Z ### Background
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5141389Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5141613Z The Azure SDK for Rust has gone through three eras:
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5142002Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5142899Z 1. **Community era (2017–2019):** MindFlavor's `azure_sdk_*` crates ([MindFlavor/AzureSDKForRust](https://github.com/MindFlavor/AzureSDKForRust))
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5144457Z 2. **Experimental era (2022–2024):** Azure-published `azure_*` crates, v0.1–0.21 (not stable)
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5145711Z 3. **Official/GA era (2025+):** Current rewrite with stable API design, new crate names for several services
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5146391Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5147302Z Many Rust developers are using crates from eras 1 or 2, or community alternatives like [`azeventhubs`]((crates.io/redacted)(crates.io/redacted) Each migration guide should cover:
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5148333Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5148685Z - What the predecessor crate was and its relationship to the official crate
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5149380Z - Key API differences and breaking changes
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5149974Z - Step-by-step migration instructions with code examples
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5150547Z - Common pitfalls and FAQ
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5150806Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5150956Z ### Sub-Issues
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5151170Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5151617Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_core` (from azure_core ≤0.21.0 / azure_sdk_core) — #TBD1
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5152671Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_identity` (from azure_identity ≤0.21.0 / azure_sdk_auth_aad) — #TBD2
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5153769Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_security_keyvault_secrets` (from azure_security_keyvault) — #TBD3
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5154850Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_security_keyvault_keys` (from azure_security_keyvault) — #TBD4
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5157396Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_security_keyvault_certificates` (from azure_security_keyvault) — #TBD5
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5158530Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_messaging_eventhubs` (from azeventhubs) — #TBD6
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5159567Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_data_cosmos` (from azure_data_cosmos ≤0.21.0 / azure_sdk_cosmos) — #TBD7
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5199462Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_storage_blob` (from azure_storage_blobs / azure_sdk_storage_blob) — #TBD8
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5200673Z - [ ] Migration guide: `azure_storage_queue` (from azure_storage_queues) — #TBD9
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5201201Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5201832Z > **Note:** Migration guide for `azure_messaging_servicebus` is deferred until the era 3 rewrite is published on crates.io.
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5202604Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5202763Z ### Priority
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5202971Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5203666Z 1. **GA crates first (April 2026):** Identity, Key Vault (Secrets, Keys, Certificates), Event Hubs, Storage Blob
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5204749Z 2. **Near-GA:** Core (infrastructure for all crates), Cosmos DB (June 2026), Storage Queue
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5231055Z 3. **Deferred:** Service Bus (era 3 rewrite not yet published)
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5232822Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5233020Z ### Acceptance Criteria
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5233337Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5233540Z - [ ] All 9 sub-issues filed and linked
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5234102Z - [ ] Each migration guide published in repo docs
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5234745Z - [ ] Each guide includes code samples for key scenarios
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5235375Z - [ ] Each guide linked from the relevant crate README
2026-04-20T23:07:55.5236802Z Redacted URL domains written to: /tmp/gh-aw/redacted-urls.log

2026-04-20T23:07:55.8083186Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8083344Z # Agentic Triage
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8083554Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8083999Z You are a triage assistant for GitHub issues. Analyze issue #4228 and perform initial triage.
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8084599Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8084792Z 1. Retrieve issue content using `get_issue`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8085132Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8085779Z    - If the issue is spam, bot-generated, or not actionable, add a one-sentence analysis comment and exit
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8086777Z    - If the issue is already assigned, has labels, or has a parent issue, exit
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8087273Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8087490Z 2. Use GitHub tools to gather additional context
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8087851Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8088271Z    - Do not run shell commands like `gh label list` - rely on labels inferred from repo context
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8089034Z    - Fetch comments using `get_issue_comments`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8089565Z    - Find similar issues using `search_issues`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8090160Z    - Find linked pull requests using `search_pull_requests`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8090746Z    - List open issues using `list_issues`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8091075Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8091233Z 3. Analyze issue content
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8091488Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8091652Z    - Title and description
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8092143Z    - Type: bug report, feature request, question, etc.
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8092679Z    - Technical areas mentioned
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8093133Z    - Severity or priority indicators
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8093593Z    - User impact
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8094035Z    - Crate names beginning with `azure` or `typespec`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8094598Z    - Changed files in linked pull requests
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8094934Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8095483Z 4. Write notes, ideas, nudges, resource links, debugging strategies, and reproduction steps relevant to the issue
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8096362Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8096609Z 5. Select appropriate labels from available repo labels
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8097133Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8097385Z    - All issues should have a #ffeb77 colored type label
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8098052Z      - `Client` - crates not starting with `azure_resourcemanager_`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8098938Z      - `Mgmt` - crates starting with `azure_resourcemanager_` or mentions of ARM or Resource Manager
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8099849Z      - `Service` - REST API or service behavior outside client SDK control
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8100778Z    - Tag issues from users without repo write access as `customer-reported` and `needs-team-attention`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8101731Z    - Tag questions (not bug reports or feature requests) with `question`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8102709Z    - Add `EngSys` service label for issues with scripts, workflows, or pipelines under /eng but not /eng/common
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8103676Z    - Use labels from similar issues for #e99695 colored service labels
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8104792Z    - If pull requests are linked to similar issues, check those pull requests' file paths against matching patterns in /.github/CODEOWNERS
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8106874Z      - If matches are found, use the `PRLabel` value in a comment above those lines (e.g. `PRLabel: %KeyVault`) to find related `ServiceLabel`s (e.g. `ServiceLabel: %KeyVault`) grouped with `AzureSDKOwners` and `ServiceOwners`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8108386Z      - Strip leading `@` from users and groups when assigning issues
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8108997Z      - Strip leading `%` from labels
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8109549Z      - Add #e99695 colored service labels from `ServiceLabel`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8110721Z      - If `Client` is applicable and there are `AzureSDKOwners`, assign to a random owner; if only `ServiceOwners` exist, add `Service Attention`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8111754Z      - Comment using this template when routing:
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8112114Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8112269Z        ```markdown
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8113139Z        Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team members best able to assist. cc {{ `AzureSDKOwners` each prefaced with `@` }}
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8114127Z        ```
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8114314Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8114691Z      - If `Service` is applicable, add applicable labels and `needs-triage`, then exit
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8115802Z    - All issues should have a #e99695 colored service label describing the relevant service
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8116919Z    - If unable to apply exactly one #ffeb77 type label and at least one #e99695 service label, apply only `needs-triage`
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8118137Z    - Add `needs-team-triage` if labels are added but `Service Attention` is not used and no person is assigned
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8118812Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8118976Z 6. Apply selected labels
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8119236Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8119421Z    - Use `update_issue` to apply labels
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8119935Z    - Do not apply labels if none clearly apply
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8120532Z    - Do not add comments beyond the markdown templates above
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8120935Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8121137Z 7. Add an issue comment with your analysis
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8121469Z 
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8121716Z    - Start with "🎯 Agentic Issue Triage"
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8122205Z    - Brief summary of the issue
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8122732Z    - Relevant details to help the team understand the issue
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8123408Z    - Debugging strategies or reproduction steps if applicable
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8124177Z    - Helpful resources or links related to the issue or affected codebase area
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8124903Z    - Nudges or ideas for addressing the issue
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8125651Z    - Break down into sub-tasks with a checklist if appropriate
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8126612Z    - Use collapsed-by-default GitHub markdown sections; collapse all sections except the short main summary
2026-04-20T23:07:55.8127296Z
